based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/external/pytorch.adam.golden.jsonl. v0.9.1 Adam/AdamW adversarial plate — each fixture surfaces a distinct attack class with a deterministic mutation.

mutation.explanation: Adam/AdamW updates REQUIRE state_before and state_after blocks per receipt.v0.5.0 schema (conditional if/then guard) and Rule 20. Dropping state_before represents the class of "operator claims Adam but ships state shape that doesn't match Adam's required (m, v) pair" — including the AMSGrad case where the operator wants a third state field (max_v / v_hat_max) but Adam's AdamState is closed-shape (additionalProperties:false). Rule 20 catches the missing state_before structurally.

reconciliation_check_targeted_first: Rule 20: Optimizer-state shape consistency — Adam/AdamW update missing required state_before block.
purpose: v0.9.1 Adam/AdamW anti-circularity fixture (kind: 'amsgrad-confusion'). Pressure-tests that the named rule fires BEFORE the reconciler consults fixture_status metadata.
v0_9_1_trust_framing: Adam-rule trust framing (load-bearing): Rules 22, 23, 24 are STRUCTURAL CONSISTENCY checks, NOT producer-authenticity checks. An attacker who controls every byte of the sidecar and recomputes a consistent (g, m, v, update) quadruple passes Rules 22-24 trivially — analogous to Rule 17's bundle-integrity caveat and the Fang et al. 2023 EuroS&P spoofing class against Proof-of-Learning (arXiv:2208.03567).
